2606.28556.title: IMCBench: A benchmark for multimodal LLMs in Image-grounded Medical Conversations
2606.28556.paper_summary: Recent advances in large language models and vision-language models have enabled reasoning over multimodal data, offering opportunities for clinical applications such as decision support and triaging. However, existing medical AI benchmarks are fragmented: some support multi-turn dialogues but lack images, while others provide multimodal inputs but focus on single-turn QA tasks. To address this gap, we introduce IMCBench, an image-grounded, multi-turn medical conversation benchmark that pairs real, publicly available clinical images with synthetic patient profiles to simulate realistic patient-clinician interactions. Each conversation is evaluated across three clinical dimensions: safety, accuracy, and appropriate use of uncertainty in diagnosis. We benchmark eight multimodal frontier models across four model families (Claude, GPT, Nova, and Llama), scoring each on a 1-5 scale using LLM-as-Jury scoring calibrated against expert clinician annotations. Our results show that Claude Opus 4.6 achieves the highest overall score (3.61), followed by Claude Sonnet 4.6 (3.30) and GPT-5.2 (3.29), though no model dominates all dimensions and safety degrades for both malignant and rare conditions ($Δ$ = -0.27 each). Ablation studies further reveal that both visual input and EHR context contribute to safe guidance (safety drops of 0.18 and 0.23 on average when each is removed), with stronger models leveraging visual features more effectively. Together, these findings demonstrate that accurate clinical description does not guarantee safe patient guidance, motivating the need for multi-dimensional evaluation frameworks in medical AI.
2606.28556.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型語言模型和視覺-語言模型方面的進展，使得對多模態數據的推理成為可能，為臨床應用提供了機會，如決策支持和分診。<lb/>然而，現有的醫療AI基準是零散的：有些支持多輪對話但缺乏圖像，而另一些則提供多模態輸入但專注於單輪問答任務。<lb/>為了解決這一差距，我們介紹了IMCBench，一個以圖像為基礎的多輪醫療對話基準，將真實的公開臨床圖像與合成的病人資料配對，以模擬現實的病人-臨床醫師互動。<lb/>每個對話在三個臨床維度上進行評估：安全性、準確性和在診斷中適當使用不確定性。<lb/>我們在四個模型家族（Claude、GPT、Nova和Llama）中對八個多模態前沿模型進行基準測試，並使用對專家臨床醫師註釋進行校準的LLM-as-Jury評分，對每個模型進行1到5的評分。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，Claude Opus 4.6獲得了最高的總分（3.61），其次是Claude Sonnet 4.6（3.30）和GPT-5.2（3.29），儘管沒有模型在所有維度上都佔據主導地位，並且在惡性和罕見病症的安全性上都有下降（$Δ$ = -0.27各自）。<lb/>消融研究進一步揭示，視覺輸入和電子健康紀錄（EHR）上下文都對安全指導有所貢獻（當去除各自時，安全性平均下降0.18和0.23），更強大的模型更有效地利用視覺特徵。<lb/>綜合這些發現表明，準確的臨床描述並不保證安全的病人指導，這促使我們在醫療AI中需要多維度的評估框架。
2606.28556.author: Maria Xenochristou et.al.
2606.28556.authors: Maria Xenochristou, Ashutosh Joshi, Korosh Vatanparvar, Mohammad Abuzar Hashemi, Prasad Kasu, Deepak Bansal, Anchal Nema, Nivedita Wadhwa, Prashams S Jain, Rebecca Abraham, Will Kimbrough, Dilek Hakkani-Tur, Wilko Schulz-Mahlendorf
